_about: Sample LLM model registry for the Puppetmaster router. Copy to ~/.puppetmaster/models.json (or run `puppetmaster models init`), then edit capability_score / prices / adapter_model_name to match your actual subscriptions. Anything in this file is user-asserted — Puppetmaster does not benchmark or fetch live prices.

_format.adapter: puppetmaster adapter name: 'claude-code', 'cursor', or a future raw HTTP adapter
_format.adapter_model_name: literal model name passed through to the adapter at runtime
_format.input_per_mtok_usd: $ per million input tokens (0 for models billed via your Cursor/Claude plan)
_format.tags: free-form tags; routing policies and the vision classifier filter on these
_format.enabled: set false to skip without deleting

models.notes: Fast/cheap tier. Cursor's house Composer model. Verification, exploration, formatting, low-stakes work. $0 because bundled with your Cursor plan.
Balanced tier. GPT-5.5 via the Cursor SDK. Same model as openai/gpt-5-5 but billed through your Cursor plan ($0 from the router's perspective).

Cheap/fast tier on the Anthropic side. The Claude-Code analogue to Cursor's composer-2.5, except it bills per-token ($1/$5 per MTok) instead of being free. Router prefers cursor/composer-2-5 or openai/gpt-5-4-nano when available.
High-quality tier. Anthropic Opus 4.6 via the Claude Code CLI. Workhorse for implementation, refactoring, and review. Pricing per the Anthropic 4.x rate schedule ($5/$25 per MTok).
Frontier tier. Anthropic Opus 4.7 via the Claude Code CLI. Best for detailed-vision, security audits, red-team work. Pricing per the Anthropic 4.x rate schedule ($5/$25 per MTok).
OpenAI frontier coding/reasoning model. 1M context, 128K max output. Routes through the openai adapter (needs OPENAI_API_KEY).
OpenAI workhorse. 1M context at half the price of GPT-5.5. Good default for implementation tasks where you don't need frontier reasoning.
OpenAI mini. 400K context. Cheap enough to run as a default for exploration and verification while staying capable.
OpenAI nano. Cheapest GPT-5 tier for high-throughput reading and trivial edits. Pricing is an estimate; update when nano pricing is public.

OpenAI Codex CLI (codex exec --json) driving gpt-5.5. Same model & billing as openai/gpt-5-5, but the Codex CLI ships an agent loop with built-in file edits, shell, and search. Slightly higher capability_score than openai/gpt-5-5 reflects the agent-loop advantage for multi-file refactors and audits.
Codex CLI driving gpt-5.4-mini. Same model & billing as openai/gpt-5-4-mini, with the Codex agent loop enabled.
